reuse_findings.reason: Table 'inventory_transfers' does not exist in baseline schema.ts. No similarly-named table found. Unit YAML cites it under reuse[] but the table has never been created. The unit must (a) declare the entire inventory_transfers table under tables_columns.new[] OR (b) declare a depends_on link to the (currently-missing) unit that creates the base table.

fuzzy_collisions.reason: Exact name 'rejected_by' already on four other tables. Acceptable reuse pattern (same semantics — FK to user/dealer who rejected) but auditor logs the parallel for review.
Exact name on three other tables — consistent rejection-timestamp idiom.
Exact name on five other tables — established naming pattern.

notes: Sync Audit G-06 (MEDIUM) — V2-Feb inventory_transfers has 2-state status ENUM with no rejection path; Sec6.S.5 specifies a reject-transfer flow. Resolution E adds three nullable rejection columns and extends the status ENUM with two values.

PRIMARY BLOCKER: the inventory_transfers table itself does not exist in the current schema.ts baseline. The YAML must add the full table to tables_columns.new[] (or add depends_on to a unit that creates it) before the rejection-column extension can be applied.

SECONDARY: the three new column names exactly match existing columns on 4-5 other tables. While this matches the repo-wide rejection idiom (likely intentional reuse of the convention), the auditor records the fuzzy collisions per rule 3 of the format spec — the loop reviewer should confirm same semantics.
All three column types parse as valid Drizzle column builders (integer / timestamp / text).

Status ENUM extension ('rejected_by_target', 'cancelled_by_admin') is not declared as a tables_columns change; if status is implemented as a Postgres ENUM type, the migration also needs an ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE statement that the YAML does not currently capture.
